request_debug.prompt_preview:
You rerank dead-startup candidates for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and K candidates retrieved by hybrid search, score each candidate from four perspectives, then return ONLY the top 5 ranked from most to least relevant.

Perspectives (each scored 0-10 with a one-sentence rationale):
- business_model: how similar are the revenue, pricing, and customer-relationship shapes?
- market: how similar are the target buyers, market size, and demand drivers?
- gtm: how similar are the go-to-

response.text: {"ranked":[{"candidate_id":"quibi::media_content","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":1,"rationale":"Quibi was a B2C consumer subscription streaming platform, entirely unlike a B2B SaaS per-driver logistics tool."},"market":{"score":1,"rationale":"Quibi targeted general consumers for entertainment, with no overlap with US regional 3PL logistics operators."},"gtm":{"score":1,"rationale":"Quibi used mass-market consumer app distribution and studio partnerships, not B2B sales to SMB logistics companies."},"stage_scale":{"score":2,"rationale":"Quibi was heavily funded at $1.75B and operated at consumer scale, far removed from an SMB-focused logistics SaaS startup."}},"rationale":"Quibi shares virtually no similarity with the pitch — different sector, business model, customer type, and GTM motion. Only marginal relevance as a failed subscription-based startup."},{"candidate_id":"blockbuster::retail_ecommerce","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":1,"rationale":"Blockbuster was a retail video rental chain with some subscription experiments, not a B2B SaaS product with per-driver pricing."},"market":{"score":1,"rationale":"Blockbuster served consumer entertainment markets, with no connection to logistics, 3PLs, or supply chain SMBs."},"gtm":{"score":1,"rationale":"Blockbuster relied on physical retail stores and later DVD-by-mail, completely unlike B2B SaaS sales to logistics operators."},"stage_scale":{"score":1,"rationale":"Blockbuster was a multi-billion dollar brick-and-mortar retail giant, not an early-stage B2B SaaS startup."}},"rationale":"Blockbuster has essentially no similarity to the pitch across all dimensions — different sector, model, market, GTM, and scale."},{"candidate_id":"wikipedia.org","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":1,"rationale":"Pebble was a B2C hardware company selling smartwatches via Kickstarter and retail, not a B2B SaaS subscription service."},"market":{"score":1,"rationale":"Pebble targeted consumer wearables buyers, with no overlap with the logistics/3PL SMB market."},"gtm":{"score":1,"rationale":"Pebble used crowdfunding and consumer retail channels, entirely unlike B2B SaaS sales to regional logistics companies."},"stage_scale":{"score":2,"rationale":"Pebble shipped ~2 million units before insolvency, representing a consumer hardware scale rather than an early B2B SaaS stage."}},"rationale":"Pebble shares no meaningful similarity to the pitch — it is a consumer hardware company with no relevance to B2B logistics SaaS."}]}
